regime_justification: USDJPY pinned at 159, MOF intervention threats loud, BOJ meeting tomorrow with no-hike consensus, oil above $95 driving imported inflation, JGB yields at multi-decade highs, but equity vol subdued—mixed signals justify carry-neutral stance

domino_stage.label: Pre-Intervention
domino_stage.justification: USDJPY at 159.17, less than 1 yen from the 160 MOF watch line; Katayama explicitly signaling 'bold action' readiness; BOJ meeting 27-28 April with elevated uncertainty; JGB 10Y spiking; risk reversals unavailable but positioning fragile

1w.label: Watchful
1w.driver: BOJ decision tomorrow (Apr 28) is close-call; no-hike expected but messaging risk high; USDJPY 0.8 yen from 160; applied unwind prior—data ambiguous on vol but setup fragile

2w.label: Watchful
2w.driver: Elevated intervention risk persists if USDJPY breaks 160; oil remains elevated near $96; spread 188 bps still wide but JGB yield climbing reduces carry cushion

3w.label: Unlikely
3w.driver: If BOJ holds and oil stabilizes per ceasefire rumors, pressure eases; MOVE & VIX both subdued signal contained stress; unwind risk recedes absent catalyst

4w.label: Unlikely
4w.driver: June BOJ meeting (Jun 14-15) priced at 57% hike probability; if oil moderates and Hormuz opens, thesis loses momentum through May

bottom_line: Stage 2 pre-intervention: USDJPY at 159.17, MOF threatening 'bold action,' BOJ holds tomorrow but messaging risk acute. Oil $96 fueling imported inflation, JGB 10Y at 2.45% (27-year high), spread still 188 bps. Equities calm (VIX 19, MOVE 67) but yen positioning fragile. Grades C/C/D/D: 1-2w watchful on BOJ/MOF surprise, 3-4w unlikely absent fresh catalyst. BTC $77.8K holding; crypto stable but would drain fast on real unwind. Watch: BOJ tone tomorrow, USDJPY pace past 160, oil trajectory.

trigger_map.note: USDJPY 159.17, 0.83 yen from 160; Katayama Apr 16 signaled 'bold action' readiness; rate-check rumors circulating; 1-2 day window
Meeting 27-28 Apr; market prices 7% hike probability; split vote in March shows internal pressure; hold expected but messaging critical

trigger_map.trigger: JGB 10Y break above prior cycle high
trigger_map.note: Now 2.45%, highest since 1999; prior cycle peak ~2.5%; another 5 bps triggers multi-decade breakout and carry unwind
Currently 67, well below threshold; rates vol calm; becomes cocked only if JGB or UST dislocate

trigger_map.trigger: Energy / Hormuz shock pushing ¥/bbl > prior peak
trigger_map.note: ¥/bbl now ¥15,360; Iran submitted Hormuz reopening proposal today but stalemate persists; 9th week of conflict; IEA calls it largest supply shock on record
No USD funding stress visible; dollar stable; trigger dormant until broader liquidity event

narrative.p1: Overnight: USDJPY printed 159.17, down 0.13% but holding firm 0.83 yen below the MOF's 160 intervention watch line. Oil spiked to $96.7 intraday on Iran stalemate before easing to $96.5 on reports Tehran submitted a Hormuz reopening proposal via Pakistan—though Trump canceled envoy visit and Iran says no talks 'under blockade.' JGB 10Y jumped 3 bps to 2.45%, nearing the prior cycle high as markets position for tomorrow's BOJ decision (Apr 27-28). US 10Y steady at 4.31%. VIX 19, MOVE 67—cross-asset vol subdued. No regime change from yesterday (yellow), but stage advances from 1 (Pressure Building) to 2 (Pre-Intervention): USDJPY at watch line, MOF rhetoric loud, BOJ meeting live, and JGB breaking out. Grade holds at C for 1w due to ambiguous BOJ messaging risk—applied unwind prior as tiebreaker (would otherwise be D on benign vol).
narrative.p2: Quant anchors: US-JP 10Y spread 186 bps (compressed 5 bps as JGB climbs faster than UST). USDJPY 1M implied vol 7.7—multi-month low, absurdly complacent given BOJ decision tomorrow. Carry-to-vol proxy ~24 (186/7.7), still elevated but eroding. USDJPY 5d pace -0.4%, well within normal range—MOF watches pace more than level, so no disorderly move yet. Distance from 60d mean: near upper band, stretched but not extreme. ¥/bbl crude ¥15,360, double hit from oil + yen. Domino Stage 2 justified: USDJPY sub-1-yen from 160, Katayama Apr 16 signaled 'bold action' readiness, BOJ meeting is close-call per ex-official Momma, and JGB yield at 27-year high. FX risk reversals unavailable (data gap). Applied unwind prior to 1w grade: BOJ hold expected (93% probability) but messaging opaque; if Ueda signals accelerated normalization or if market misreads dovish hold as capitulation, positioning could snap. Prior leans one notch more alert (C vs D). 2w also C on sustained intervention risk. 3-4w downgrade to D: if BOJ navigates cleanly and oil moderates, pressure dissipates into June meeting (priced 57% hike).
narrative.p3: Crypto: BTC $77.8K, flat on day, +6.4% over 7d. Risk-on holding; consolidation after bounce from $74K low. Open interest data unavailable, but price action suggests modest inflows as equities grind higher (S&P +0.8% Friday). BTC-DXY correlation unavailable. XRP $1.42, +0.8%. Interpretation: crypto is a lagging liquidity gauge, not leading. Current stability reflects contained cross-asset vol (VIX 19, MOVE 67) and no forced deleveraging—yet. If MOF intervenes or BOJ surprises hawkish tomorrow, USDJPY could gap 2-3 yen intraday, spiking FX vol and hammering levered carry positions. That would drain crypto fast as collateral calls hit and risk-off cascades. BTC sub-$70K within 48 hours in that scenario. Conversely, if BOJ holds cleanly and oil eases, BTC has room to $80-82K as the unwind thesis fades. For now: stable, but BTC is a sympathy trade to yen carry—watch it as a real-time stress gauge, not a safe haven.

horizon_rationale.w1_w2: 1w elevated (C) because BOJ decision lands Apr 28 and messaging is opaque; even a hold can trigger volatility if Ueda signals faster normalization or if market interprets dovish tone as capitulation, compressing spread further. USDJPY 0.8 yen from 160 means any hawkish surprise or intervention talk spikes vol immediately. 2w remains C because intervention risk persists through early May if USDJPY breaks 160 or pace accelerates; oil above $95 sustains imported inflation and yen pressure.
horizon_rationale.w2_w3: 2w at C, 3w drops to D because if BOJ navigates cleanly (hold + balanced forward guidance) and Iran ceasefire rumors materialize (oil retreat), the acute catalyst window closes. MOVE/VIX both subdued, no cascade mechanics visible. By week 3, market refocuses on June BOJ meeting (Jun 14-15), currently priced 57% hike—pressure deferred, not eliminated, but unwind risk recedes absent fresh shock.
horizon_rationale.w3_w4: 3w and 4w both D (unlikely). Scenario-weighted: base case is BOJ holds, oil stabilizes $85-90 range if Hormuz partially reopens, USDJPY drifts 156-160 through May as carry persists but spread compresses slowly. June meeting becomes the next binary event. Tail risk: if Iran conflict escalates or BOJ surprises hawkish, 3-4w grades flip to B, but current probability <20%. Non-monotonic path valid: acute risk weeks 1-2, eases weeks 3-4 as catalysts resolve.

watch_list: BOJ policy statement & Ueda press conference tone Apr 28 (hold expected, but any signal of accelerated normalization or June hike pre-commitment moves USDJPY instantly)
USDJPY pace and level: break above 160 or daily move >0.5% triggers MOF action; sub-159 eases pressure
WTI crude and Iran-US talks: Hormuz reopening proposal in play; oil retreat to $85-90 removes half the yen pressure; stalemate continuation keeps ¥/bbl elevated
JGB 10Y above 2.50%: prior cycle high; breakout signals bond vigilantes stirring and accelerates carry unwind math
VIX above 22 or MOVE above 80: cross-asset stress confirmation; currently latent but would amplify any yen shock into broader risk-off
